description_long: This object consists of three croissants arranged side by side, each displaying a distinct color and finish. The left croissant is pale, resembling a classic plain or unbaked croissant, with a matte, soft surface. The center croissant is golden brown, with a glossy, appetizing sheen and visible curved layers, typical of a well-baked butter croissant. The right croissant is dark brown, almost chocolate in appearance, with a slightly less glossy finish and a denser look. All three croissants have the characteristic crescent shape, with gently tapered ends and a plump, layered middle. The surfaces show subtle ridges and folds, indicating the laminated dough structure. No visible toppings or fillings are present on any of the croissants.
description: Three croissants in pale, golden brown, and dark brown hues are arranged side by side, each with a crescent shape and layered, slightly glossy surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three croissants side by side, highlighting their color differences and crescent shapes.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the rounded tops and the alignment of the croissants, with their ends pointing toward and away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of the croissants, emphasizing their plumpness and the layering of the dough.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view revealing the undersides and the tapering ends of the croissants, with the layering less visible.
attribute_pastry_type: classic butter croissant, unbaked croissant, chocolate croissant
